old_claim.note: Market Price Bulletins provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Contract Law supplies the obligation, payment, debt, and remedy framework for clothiers' relationships with dependent out-workers.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract says the chapter examines the relationship between late-medieval clothiers and dependent out-workers, including growth in textile-related debt litigation in the Court of Common Pleas.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source frames putting-out through clothier/out-worker relationships and textile-related debt litigation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Contract law fits the obligations, debts, and remedies visible in the source; market price bulletins are not documented as the direct enabling mechanism.

ontology_before: Putting-Out Textile System depended on Market Price Bulletins, overfitting a price-publication mechanism not evidenced by the source.
ontology_after: Putting-Out Textile System depends on Contract Law for the obligation and debt framework between clothiers and dependent out-workers.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: putting_out_textile_system now depends on contract_law and no longer directly depends on market_price_bulletins.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to printed market-price reporting rather than domestic textile out-work.
A stronger source showed market price bulletins were the documented mechanism behind the late-medieval putting-out system.
The contract_law node were narrowed away from debts, hiring, obligations, and remedies.
short_reason: Putting-out obligations fit contract law, not market price bulletins.
